name: Magentic (LLM Function Decorator)

auth.notes: OPENAI_API_KEY; supports OpenAI, Anthropic, LiteLLM backends

performance.notes: LLM call latency; minimal Python overhead from decorators

agent_readiness.notes: Seamlessly integrate LLMs into Python using decorators. @prompt decorator turns a function docstring into an LLM prompt. Return type annotation drives structured output (uses Pydantic). @chatprompt for multi-turn conversations. Streaming support with AsyncIterator. FunctionCall for tool use. Works with OpenAI function calling natively. Minimal boilerplate — type annotations do the work. Elegant API for typed LLM outputs.

gotchas: Relies on OpenAI function calling for structured output — provider-specific
Complex nested Pydantic models may not parse reliably
Limited debugging tools compared to LangChain
best_when: Typed LLM outputs with Pydantic validation
Simple to medium complexity LLM apps
avoid_when: Non-OpenAI providers needing full feature parity
Production apps requiring stable long-term API
summary: Magentic uses Python decorators (@prompt, @chatprompt) to integrate LLMs with minimal boilerplate. Return type annotations (Pydantic models) drive structured output via OpenAI function calling. Streaming, async, and tool use supported. Extremely clean API — type annotations define both the LLM output format and validation. Best for typed, structured LLM outputs in Python without the verbosity of LangChain or raw instructor usage.
